2026-04-23T15:21:28.5334926Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5335495Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5335933Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5336301Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5336611Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5336845Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5337004Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5337182Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5337349Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5337577Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5337778Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5338063Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T15:21:28.5338318Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). The package covers VM lifecycle management on Azure Stack HCI clusters, including virtual machine instances, virtual hard disks, gallery images, logical networks, storage containers, network interfaces, and security groups.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `README.md` Examples section contains only a generic link to `.NET Management Library Code Samples` — no package-specific code snippets whatsoever\\n- The test directory `tests/Samples/` contains 21 auto-generated sample files (VM instance CRUD, VHD management, gallery image management, logical network management, storage container management, network interface management, security group management), none of which are surfaced in the README\\n- No `samples/Readme.cs` file exists to back README snippet placeholders (unlike the sibling `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci` package which has `samples/Readme.cs`)\\n- The `Key concepts` section is a stub linking only to generic SDK docs — no description of HCI VM-specific resource model (VmInstance, LogicalNetwork, VirtualHardDisk, etc.)\\n- The package description in the csproj (`Azure Resource Manager client SDK for Azure resource provider Hci.Vm`) is generic and doesn\\u0026#39;t describe the service\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nReplace the `## Examples` section with the following (the snippet bodies will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after Step 2):\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm` lets you manage virtual machine workloads running on Azure Stack HCI clusters. The key resource types are:\\n\\n- **HciVmInstance** – A virtual machine instance running on a HCI node (child resource of an Arc machine).\\n- **HciVmVirtualHardDisk** – A managed virtual hard disk stored on a HCI storage path.\\n- **HciVmLogicalNetwork** – A logical (VLAN/overlay) network on a HCI cluster.\\n- **HciVmStorageContainer** – A storage container (SMB share or local path) for placing VM disks and images.\\n- **HciVmGalleryImage** / **HciVmMarketplaceGalleryImage** – VM OS images sourced from a local gallery or Azure Marketplace.\\n- **HciVmNetworkInterface** – A virtual NIC attached to a logical network.\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Authenticate the client\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a virtual machine instance\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a virtual machine instance\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List all virtual machine instances under a host machine\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Delete a virtual machine instance\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a virtual hard disk\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a logical network\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/samples/Readme.cs`** — Create\\n\\nCreate this file to back the README snippet placeholders above. Use the patterns from `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/samples/Readme.cs` and the existing generated samples in `tests/Samples/` as a reference. A minimal starting point:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Readme\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task AuthClient()\\n        {\\n            #region HciVm_Readme_AuthClient\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetVmInstance()\\n        {\\n            #region HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            // The resource URI is the Arc machine resource ID that hosts the VM\\n            string resourceUri = \\u0026#34;subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines/{machineName}\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier hciVmInstanceResourceId = HciVmInstanceResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(resourceUri);\\n            HciVmInstanceResource hciVmInstance = client.GetHciVmInstanceResource(hciVmInstanceResourceId);\\n\\n            HciVmInstanceResource result = await hciVmInstance.GetAsync();\\n            HciVmInstanceData resourceData = result.Data;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;VM Instance id: {resourceData.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task DeleteVmInstance()\\n        {\\n            #region HciVm_Readme_DeleteVmInstance\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string resourceUri = \\u0026#34;subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines/{machineName}\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier hciVmInstanceResourceId = HciVmInstanceResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(resourceUri);\\n            HciVmInstanceResource hciVmInstance = client.GetHciVmInstanceResource(hciVmInstanceResourceId);\\n\\n            await hciVmInstance.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine(\\u0026#34;VM instance deleted.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** The `CreateVmInstance`, `ListVmInstances`, `CreateVhd`, and `CreateLogicalNetwork` snippets should be authored similarly, drawing from the existing generated samples in `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmInstanceResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmVirtualHardDiskCollection.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmLogicalNetworkCollection.cs`.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. Write the sample code in `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/samples/Readme.cs` (created in Step 1) with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers as shown above.\\n\\n2. Add snippet placeholders in `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md`:\\n\\n   ````\\n   ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance\\n   ```\\n   ````\\n\\n   The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will replace the empty placeholder bodies with the actual code.\\n\\n3. Snippet names must match exactly between the test file region and the README placeholder:\\n   - `HciVm_Readme_AuthClient`\\n   - `HciVm_Readme_CreateVmInstance`\\n   - `HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance`\\n   - `HciVm_Readme_ListVmInstances`\\n   - `HciVm_Readme_DeleteVmInstance`\\n   - `HciVm_Readme_CreateVhd`\\n   - `HciVm_Readme_CreateLogicalNetwork`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md` is mostly structured correctly but needs these updates:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Stub | Replace with HCI VM resource model description (see Step 1) |\\n| Examples | ❌ Empty | Add snippet placeholders (see Steps 1 \\u0026amp; 2) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 azurestackhci` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from sample files and injects code into README.md placeholder blocks; verify with `git diff` that README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 azurestackhci` — only needed if public API surface changed\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/` — ensures consistent formatting\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
